name: Clams (Whole or Minced)
recipeIngredient: Whole clams, live on ice until ready to use
Miniature clams, live on ice until ready to use
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub shells thoroughly and rinse.
2. Steam clams for 5 minutes.
3. Open the shells and remove clam meat.
4. Collect and save clam juice.
5. Wash clam meat in water containing 1 teaspoon of salt per quart.
6. Rinse clam meat and cover with boiling water containing 2 tablespoons of lemon juice or 1/2 teaspoon of citric acid per gallon.
7. Boil for 2 minutes, then drain.
8. To make minced clams, grind the clam meat using a meat grinder or food processor.
9. Fill jars loosely with pieces of minced clams.
10. Add hot clam juice and boiling water if needed, leaving 1-inch headspace.
11. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 or Table 2 in the recipe.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Clams in a dial-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Half-pints, Pints
- Process Time: 60 minutes for Half-pints, 70 minutes for Pints
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
Table 2: Recommended process time for Clams in a weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Jar Size: Half-pints, Pints
- Process Time: 60 minutes for Half-pints, 70 minutes for Pints
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb. Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
